title: Exercises: Quantum Layers
description: Practice implementing and analyzing different quantum layer architectures

exercises.title: Hardware-Efficient Layer Implementation
exercises.description: Implement a hardware-efficient layer with RY, RZ rotations and linear CNOT entanglement.
hints: Apply RY then RZ to each qubit
Add CNOT gates between adjacent qubit pairs
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

class HardwareEfficientLayer:
    """Hardware-efficient quantum layer."""
    
    def __init__(self, n_qubits):
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.dim = 2 ** n_qubits
        self.n_params = 2 * n_qubits  # RY + RZ per qubit
    
    def _ry(self, theta, qubit):
        """Build RY gate matrix."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
        
        for z in range(self.dim):
            z_flip = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit))
            if z < z_flip:
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z, z_flip] = -s
                U[z_flip, z] = s
                U[z_flip, z_flip] = c
        return U
    
    def _rz(self, theta, qubit):
        """Build RZ gate matrix."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit)) & 1
            phase = np.exp(-1j * theta/2) if bit == 0 else np.exp(1j * theta/2)
            U[z, z] = phase
        return U
    
    def _cnot(self, control, target):
        """Build CNOT gate matrix."""
        U = np.zeros((self.dim, self.dim), dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            ctrl_bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - control)) & 1
            if ctrl_bit:
                z_new = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - target))
            else:
                z_new = z
            U[z_new, z] = 1
        return U
    
    def build(self, params):
        """Build the layer unitary.
        
        Args:
            params: Array of 2*n_qubits parameters
            
        Returns:
            Unitary matrix for the layer
        """
        # TODO: Start with identity matrix
        # TODO: Apply RY(params[2*q]) and RZ(params[2*q+1]) to each qubit q
        # TODO: Apply CNOT between adjacent qubits (0-1, 1-2, etc.)
        # TODO: Return the composed unitary
        pass

# Test
print("Hardware-Efficient Layer Test")
print("=" * 50)

layer = HardwareEfficientLayer(n_qubits=3)
params = np.array([0.5, 0.3, 0.7, 0.2, 0.9, 0.4])  # 6 params for 3 qubits

U = layer.build(params)

print(f"Number of qubits: {layer.n_qubits}")
print(f"Parameters: {layer.n_params}")
print(f"Is unitary: {np.allclose(U @ U.conj().T, np.eye(8))}")

# Apply to |000>
state = np.zeros(8, dtype=complex)
state[0] = 1
output = U @ state

print("\nOutput probabilities:")
for z in range(8):
    prob = np.abs(output[z])**2
    if prob > 0.01:
        print(f"  |{format(z, '03b')}>: {prob:.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

class HardwareEfficientLayer:
    """Hardware-efficient quantum layer."""
    
    def __init__(self, n_qubits):
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.dim = 2 ** n_qubits
        self.n_params = 2 * n_qubits  # RY + RZ per qubit
    
    def _ry(self, theta, qubit):
        """Build RY gate matrix."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
        
        for z in range(self.dim):
            z_flip = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit))
            if z < z_flip:
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z, z_flip] = -s
                U[z_flip, z] = s
                U[z_flip, z_flip] = c
        return U
    
    def _rz(self, theta, qubit):
        """Build RZ gate matrix."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit)) & 1
            phase = np.exp(-1j * theta/2) if bit == 0 else np.exp(1j * theta/2)
            U[z, z] = phase
        return U
    
    def _cnot(self, control, target):
        """Build CNOT gate matrix."""
        U = np.zeros((self.dim, self.dim), dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            ctrl_bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - control)) & 1
            if ctrl_bit:
                z_new = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - target))
            else:
                z_new = z
            U[z_new, z] = 1
        return U
    
    def build(self, params):
        """Build the layer unitary.
        
        Args:
            params: Array of 2*n_qubits parameters
            
        Returns:
            Unitary matrix for the layer
        """
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        
        # Rotation layer
        for q in range(self.n_qubits):
            U = self._ry(params[2*q], q) @ U
            U = self._rz(params[2*q + 1], q) @ U
        
        # Linear entanglement
        for q in range(self.n_qubits - 1):
            U = self._cnot(q, q + 1) @ U
        
        return U

# Test
print("Hardware-Efficient Layer Test")
print("=" * 50)

layer = HardwareEfficientLayer(n_qubits=3)
params = np.array([0.5, 0.3, 0.7, 0.2, 0.9, 0.4])  # 6 params for 3 qubits

U = layer.build(params)

print(f"Number of qubits: {layer.n_qubits}")
print(f"Parameters: {layer.n_params}")
print(f"Is unitary: {np.allclose(U @ U.conj().T, np.eye(8))}")

# Apply to |000>
state = np.zeros(8, dtype=complex)
state[0] = 1
output = U @ state

print("\nOutput probabilities:")
for z in range(8):
    prob = np.abs(output[z])**2
    if prob > 0.01:
        print(f"  |{format(z, '03b')}>: {prob:.4f}")

exercises.title: Strongly Entangling Layer with All-to-All CZ
exercises.description: Implement a strongly entangling layer with RX, RY, RZ rotations and all-to-all CZ gates.
hints: Apply three rotations (RX, RY, RZ) to each qubit
CZ gate adds -1 phase only when both qubits are |1>
All-to-all means CZ between every pair (i,j) where i < j
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

class StronglyEntanglingLayer:
    """Strongly entangling layer with all-to-all connectivity."""
    
    def __init__(self, n_qubits):
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.dim = 2 ** n_qubits
        self.n_params = 3 * n_qubits  # RX, RY, RZ per qubit
        self.n_cz = n_qubits * (n_qubits - 1) // 2
    
    def _rx(self, theta, qubit):
        """RX gate."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            z_flip = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit))
            if z < z_flip:
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z, z_flip] = -1j * s
                U[z_flip, z] = -1j * s
                U[z_flip, z_flip] = c
        return U
    
    def _ry(self, theta, qubit):
        """RY gate."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            z_flip = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit))
            if z < z_flip:
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z, z_flip] = -s
                U[z_flip, z] = s
                U[z_flip, z_flip] = c
        return U
    
    def _rz(self, theta, qubit):
        """RZ gate."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit)) & 1
            phase = np.exp(-1j * theta/2) if bit == 0 else np.exp(1j * theta/2)
            U[z, z] = phase
        return U
    
    def _cz(self, q1, q2):
        """CZ gate."""
        # TODO: Implement CZ gate
        # CZ applies -1 phase only when both qubits are |1>
        pass
    
    def build(self, params):
        """Build the layer unitary.
        
        Args:
            params: Array of 3*n_qubits parameters
            
        Returns:
            Unitary matrix
        """
        # TODO: Apply RX, RY, RZ to each qubit
        # TODO: Apply CZ between all pairs (q1, q2) where q1 < q2
        pass

# Test
print("Strongly Entangling Layer Test")
print("=" * 50)

layer = StronglyEntanglingLayer(n_qubits=3)
params = np.random.uniform(0, np.pi, layer.n_params)

U = layer.build(params)

print(f"Qubits: {layer.n_qubits}")
print(f"Parameters: {layer.n_params}")
print(f"CZ gates: {layer.n_cz}")
print(f"Is unitary: {np.allclose(U @ U.conj().T, np.eye(8))}")

# Compare gate counts
print(f"\nComparison with hardware-efficient (3 qubits):")
print(f"  HE params: 6, CNOTs: 2")
print(f"  SE params: {layer.n_params}, CZs: {layer.n_cz}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

class StronglyEntanglingLayer:
    """Strongly entangling layer with all-to-all connectivity."""
    
    def __init__(self, n_qubits):
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.dim = 2 ** n_qubits
        self.n_params = 3 * n_qubits  # RX, RY, RZ per qubit
        self.n_cz = n_qubits * (n_qubits - 1) // 2
    
    def _rx(self, theta, qubit):
        """RX gate."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            z_flip = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit))
            if z < z_flip:
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z, z_flip] = -1j * s
                U[z_flip, z] = -1j * s
                U[z_flip, z_flip] = c
        return U
    
    def _ry(self, theta, qubit):
        """RY gate."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            z_flip = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit))
            if z < z_flip:
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z, z_flip] = -s
                U[z_flip, z] = s
                U[z_flip, z_flip] = c
        return U
    
    def _rz(self, theta, qubit):
        """RZ gate."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit)) & 1
            phase = np.exp(-1j * theta/2) if bit == 0 else np.exp(1j * theta/2)
            U[z, z] = phase
        return U
    
    def _cz(self, q1, q2):
        """CZ gate."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit1 = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - q1)) & 1
            bit2 = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - q2)) & 1
            if bit1 == 1 and bit2 == 1:
                U[z, z] = -1
        return U
    
    def build(self, params):
        """Build the layer unitary.
        
        Args:
            params: Array of 3*n_qubits parameters
            
        Returns:
            Unitary matrix
        """
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        
        # Full rotation layer
        for q in range(self.n_qubits):
            U = self._rx(params[3*q], q) @ U
            U = self._ry(params[3*q + 1], q) @ U
            U = self._rz(params[3*q + 2], q) @ U
        
        # All-to-all CZ entanglement
        for q1 in range(self.n_qubits):
            for q2 in range(q1 + 1, self.n_qubits):
                U = self._cz(q1, q2) @ U
        
        return U

# Test
print("Strongly Entangling Layer Test")
print("=" * 50)

layer = StronglyEntanglingLayer(n_qubits=3)
params = np.random.uniform(0, np.pi, layer.n_params)

U = layer.build(params)

print(f"Qubits: {layer.n_qubits}")
print(f"Parameters: {layer.n_params}")
print(f"CZ gates: {layer.n_cz}")
print(f"Is unitary: {np.allclose(U @ U.conj().T, np.eye(8))}")

# Compare gate counts
print(f"\nComparison with hardware-efficient (3 qubits):")
print(f"  HE params: 6, CNOTs: 2")
print(f"  SE params: {layer.n_params}, CZs: {layer.n_cz}")

exercises.title: Particle-Number Conserving Layer
exercises.description: Implement a layer that preserves the total number of excitations (ones in the computational basis).
hints: RZ rotations preserve particle number (only add phases)
Leave |00> and |11> unchanged in the two-qubit gate
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

class ParticleConservingLayer:
    """Layer that conserves particle number."""
    
    def __init__(self, n_qubits):
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.dim = 2 ** n_qubits
        # RZ per qubit + excitation gate per adjacent pair
        self.n_params = n_qubits + (n_qubits - 1)
    
    def _rz(self, theta, qubit):
        """RZ gate (preserves particle number)."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit)) & 1
            phase = np.exp(-1j * theta/2) if bit == 0 else np.exp(1j * theta/2)
            U[z, z] = phase
        return U
    
    def _excitation_gate(self, theta, q1, q2):
        """Excitation-preserving two-qubit gate.
        
        Acts as rotation in |01>, |10> subspace.
        Leaves |00> and |11> unchanged.
        """
        # TODO: Implement excitation-preserving gate
        # For basis states |01> and |10>:
        #   |01> -> cos(theta/2)|01> + sin(theta/2)|10>
        #   |10> -> -sin(theta/2)|01> + cos(theta/2)|10>
        # |00> and |11> are unchanged
        pass
    
    def build(self, params):
        """Build the layer unitary."""
        # TODO: Apply RZ to each qubit
        # TODO: Apply excitation gates between adjacent pairs
        pass
    
    def count_particles(self, state):
        """Count expected particle number."""
        probs = np.abs(state) ** 2
        total = 0
        for z in range(self.dim):
            n_ones = bin(z).count('1')
            total += probs[z] * n_ones
        return total

# Test
print("Particle-Conserving Layer Test")
print("=" * 50)

layer = ParticleConservingLayer(n_qubits=4)
params = np.random.uniform(0, np.pi, layer.n_params)

# Test with |0011> (2 particles)
initial = np.zeros(16, dtype=complex)
initial[3] = 1  # |0011>

U = layer.build(params)
final = U @ initial

initial_n = layer.count_particles(initial)
final_n = layer.count_particles(final)

print(f"Initial state: |0011> (2 particles)")
print(f"Initial particle count: {initial_n:.4f}")
print(f"Final particle count: {final_n:.4f}")
print(f"Conservation verified: {np.isclose(initial_n, final_n)}")

# Test with different particle numbers
print("\nTesting various particle sectors:")
for target_n in [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]:
    for z in range(16):
        if bin(z).count('1') == target_n:
            state = np.zeros(16, dtype=complex)
            state[z] = 1
            output = U @ state
            out_n = layer.count_particles(output)
            print(f"  n={target_n}: in={target_n:.0f}, out={out_n:.4f}, OK={np.isclose(target_n, out_n)}")
            break
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

class ParticleConservingLayer:
    """Layer that conserves particle number."""
    
    def __init__(self, n_qubits):
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.dim = 2 ** n_qubits
        # RZ per qubit + excitation gate per adjacent pair
        self.n_params = n_qubits + (n_qubits - 1)
    
    def _rz(self, theta, qubit):
        """RZ gate (preserves particle number)."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - qubit)) & 1
            phase = np.exp(-1j * theta/2) if bit == 0 else np.exp(1j * theta/2)
            U[z, z] = phase
        return U
    
    def _excitation_gate(self, theta, q1, q2):
        """Excitation-preserving two-qubit gate.
        
        Acts as rotation in |01>, |10> subspace.
        Leaves |00> and |11> unchanged.
        """
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
        
        for z in range(self.dim):
            bit1 = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - q1)) & 1
            bit2 = (z >> (self.n_qubits - 1 - q2)) & 1
            
            if bit1 == 0 and bit2 == 1:
                # |01> state
                z_swap = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - q1)) ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - q2))
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z_swap, z] = s
            elif bit1 == 1 and bit2 == 0:
                # |10> state
                z_swap = z ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - q1)) ^ (1 << (self.n_qubits - 1 - q2))
                U[z, z] = c
                U[z_swap, z] = -s
            # |00> and |11> remain unchanged (identity)
        
        return U
    
    def build(self, params):
        """Build the layer unitary."""
        U = np.eye(self.dim, dtype=complex)
        param_idx = 0
        
        # RZ rotations
        for q in range(self.n_qubits):
            U = self._rz(params[param_idx], q) @ U
            param_idx += 1
        
        # Excitation-preserving gates
        for q in range(self.n_qubits - 1):
            U = self._excitation_gate(params[param_idx], q, q + 1) @ U
            param_idx += 1
        
        return U
    
    def count_particles(self, state):
        """Count expected particle number."""
        probs = np.abs(state) ** 2
        total = 0
        for z in range(self.dim):
            n_ones = bin(z).count('1')
            total += probs[z] * n_ones
        return total

# Test
print("Particle-Conserving Layer Test")
print("=" * 50)

layer = ParticleConservingLayer(n_qubits=4)
params = np.random.uniform(0, np.pi, layer.n_params)

# Test with |0011> (2 particles)
initial = np.zeros(16, dtype=complex)
initial[3] = 1  # |0011>

U = layer.build(params)
final = U @ initial

initial_n = layer.count_particles(initial)
final_n = layer.count_particles(final)

print(f"Initial state: |0011> (2 particles)")
print(f"Initial particle count: {initial_n:.4f}")
print(f"Final particle count: {final_n:.4f}")
print(f"Conservation verified: {np.isclose(initial_n, final_n)}")

# Test with different particle numbers
print("\nTesting various particle sectors:")
for target_n in [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]:
    for z in range(16):
        if bin(z).count('1') == target_n:
            state = np.zeros(16, dtype=complex)
            state[z] = 1
            output = U @ state
            out_n = layer.count_particles(output)
            print(f"  n={target_n}: in={target_n:.0f}, out={out_n:.4f}, OK={np.isclose(target_n, out_n)}")
            break

exercises.title: Expressibility Comparison
exercises.description: Implement a function to compare expressibility of different layer architectures.
hints: Sample random parameter pairs and compute fidelity between output states
Haar random mean fidelity is 1/(d+1) where d = 2^n
Higher expressibility means fidelity distribution closer to Haar random
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def measure_expressibility(layer_builder, n_params, dim, n_samples=300):
    """Measure expressibility of a quantum layer.
    
    Args:
        layer_builder: Function that takes params and returns unitary
        n_params: Number of parameters for the layer
        dim: Hilbert space dimension
        n_samples: Number of random samples
        
    Returns:
        Dict with expressibility metrics
    """
    # TODO: Sample n_samples pairs of random parameters
    # TODO: For each pair, compute output states from |0...0>
    # TODO: Compute fidelity (|<psi1|psi2>|^2) for each pair
    # TODO: Compare mean fidelity to Haar reference (1/(dim+1))
    # TODO: Return metrics dict
    pass

def compare_layers(n_qubits):
    """Compare different layer architectures."""
    dim = 2 ** n_qubits
    
    # Define layer builders (simplified versions)
    def he_layer(params):
        """Simple hardware-efficient layer."""
        U = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
        for q in range(n_qubits):
            theta = params[q]
            # RY rotation
            gate = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
            c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
            for z in range(dim):
                z_flip = z ^ (1 << (n_qubits - 1 - q))
                if z < z_flip:
                    gate[z, z] = c
                    gate[z, z_flip] = -s
                    gate[z_flip, z] = s
                    gate[z_flip, z_flip] = c
            U = gate @ U
        return U
    
    def se_layer(params):
        """Simple strongly entangling layer."""
        U = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
        # More rotations per qubit
        for q in range(n_qubits):
            for r in range(3):  # RX, RY, RZ
                theta = params[3*q + r]
                gate = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
                c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
                for z in range(dim):
                    z_flip = z ^ (1 << (n_qubits - 1 - q))
                    if z < z_flip:
                        gate[z, z] = c
                        gate[z, z_flip] = -s
                        gate[z_flip, z] = s
                        gate[z_flip, z_flip] = c
                U = gate @ U
        # Add CZ gates
        for q1 in range(n_qubits):
            for q2 in range(q1+1, n_qubits):
                gate = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
                for z in range(dim):
                    b1 = (z >> (n_qubits-1-q1)) & 1
                    b2 = (z >> (n_qubits-1-q2)) & 1
                    if b1 == 1 and b2 == 1:
                        gate[z, z] = -1
                U = gate @ U
        return U
    
    # TODO: Use measure_expressibility to compare layers
    # TODO: Print comparison results
    pass

# Test
print("Expressibility Comparison")
print("=" * 60)

compare_layers(n_qubits=4)
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def measure_expressibility(layer_builder, n_params, dim, n_samples=300):
    """Measure expressibility of a quantum layer.
    
    Args:
        layer_builder: Function that takes params and returns unitary
        n_params: Number of parameters for the layer
        dim: Hilbert space dimension
        n_samples: Number of random samples
        
    Returns:
        Dict with expressibility metrics
    """
    fidelities = []
    
    # Initial state |0...0>
    state0 = np.zeros(dim, dtype=complex)
    state0[0] = 1
    
    for _ in range(n_samples):
        # Sample two random parameter sets
        params1 = np.random.uniform(0, 2*np.pi, n_params)
        params2 = np.random.uniform(0, 2*np.pi, n_params)
        
        # Build unitaries and get output states
        U1 = layer_builder(params1)
        U2 = layer_builder(params2)
        
        state1 = U1 @ state0
        state2 = U2 @ state0
        
        # Compute fidelity
        fidelity = np.abs(np.vdot(state1, state2)) ** 2
        fidelities.append(fidelity)
    
    fidelities = np.array(fidelities)
    
    # Haar random reference
    haar_mean = 1 / (dim + 1)
    
    # Expressibility score (1 = Haar random, 0 = poor)
    mean_f = np.mean(fidelities)
    score = max(0, 1 - abs(mean_f - haar_mean) / haar_mean)
    
    return {
        'mean_fidelity': mean_f,
        'std_fidelity': np.std(fidelities),
        'haar_mean': haar_mean,
        'expressibility_score': score
    }

def compare_layers(n_qubits):
    """Compare different layer architectures."""
    dim = 2 ** n_qubits
    
    # Define layer builders (simplified versions)
    def he_layer(params):
        """Simple hardware-efficient layer."""
        U = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
        for q in range(n_qubits):
            theta = params[q]
            # RY rotation
            gate = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
            c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
            for z in range(dim):
                z_flip = z ^ (1 << (n_qubits - 1 - q))
                if z < z_flip:
                    gate[z, z] = c
                    gate[z, z_flip] = -s
                    gate[z_flip, z] = s
                    gate[z_flip, z_flip] = c
            U = gate @ U
        return U
    
    def se_layer(params):
        """Simple strongly entangling layer."""
        U = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
        # More rotations per qubit
        for q in range(n_qubits):
            for r in range(3):  # RX, RY, RZ
                theta = params[3*q + r]
                gate = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
                c, s = np.cos(theta/2), np.sin(theta/2)
                for z in range(dim):
                    z_flip = z ^ (1 << (n_qubits - 1 - q))
                    if z < z_flip:
                        gate[z, z] = c
                        gate[z, z_flip] = -s
                        gate[z_flip, z] = s
                        gate[z_flip, z_flip] = c
                U = gate @ U
        # Add CZ gates
        for q1 in range(n_qubits):
            for q2 in range(q1+1, n_qubits):
                gate = np.eye(dim, dtype=complex)
                for z in range(dim):
                    b1 = (z >> (n_qubits-1-q1)) & 1
                    b2 = (z >> (n_qubits-1-q2)) & 1
                    if b1 == 1 and b2 == 1:
                        gate[z, z] = -1
                U = gate @ U
        return U
    
    # Measure expressibility
    print(f"Comparing layers with {n_qubits} qubits (dim={dim})")
    print(f"Haar reference mean fidelity: {1/(dim+1):.4f}")
    print()
    
    layers = [
        ('Hardware-Efficient', he_layer, n_qubits),
        ('Strongly-Entangling', se_layer, 3 * n_qubits)
    ]
    
    print(f"{'Layer':<25} {'Params':<10} {'Mean F':<12} {'Score':<10}")
    print("-" * 57)
    
    for name, builder, n_params in layers:
        result = measure_expressibility(builder, n_params, dim, n_samples=300)
        print(f"{name:<25} {n_params:<10} {result['mean_fidelity']:<12.4f} "
              f"{result['expressibility_score']:<10.3f}")

# Test
print("Expressibility Comparison")
print("=" * 60)

compare_layers(n_qubits=4)
